Tree exhibits an approximate 15–20° lean toward the rear of the dwelling, directly over a primary target structure. A large area of visible decay is present in the mid-section of the trunk, indicating structural deterioration and increased potential for failure. Both pine trees are located less than two (2) feet from the structural foundation of the main dwelling, measured from the base of the trunk. Due to the extreme proximity to the structure, the trees present a potential hazard and create a severely limited fall zone in the event of structural failure.
